Content-Security-Policy.description: Prevents XSS and data injection attacks by specifying allowed content sources
Content-Security-Policy.remediation: Add CSP header. Start with: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

X-Frame-Options.description: Prevents clickjacking by controlling whether the page can be embedded in iframes
X-Frame-Options.remediation: Add header: X-Frame-Options: DENY (or SAMEORIGIN if iframing is needed)

X-Content-Type-Options.description: Prevents MIME-type sniffing which can lead to XSS via content-type confusion

Strict-Transport-Security.description: Forces browsers to use HTTPS, preventing protocol downgrade attacks

Referrer-Policy.description: Controls how much referrer information is sent with requests to other origins

Permissions-Policy.description: Controls which browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation) the page can use
Permissions-Policy.remediation: Add header: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

Server.description: Reveals web server software and version, aiding targeted attacks
Server.remediation: Remove or obfuscate the Server header in web server configuration

X-Powered-By.description: Reveals backend framework/language, narrowing attacker reconnaissance
X-Powered-By.remediation: Remove X-Powered-By header. In Express: app.disable('x-powered-by')
